concise_definition: Slot cut : Action that occurs when an offensive player cuts toward the basket via one of the slot areas generally located between the top and wing.

sections.heading: Basic definition
sections.text: The slot cut consists of action that happens when an offensive player cuts toward the basket via one of the slot areas generally located between the top and wing.

sections.heading: Basic examples
sections.text:
This section includes basic examples of the slot cut utilized by certain offensive players on the court.

Additionally, each of the players on the diagrams within this section are generally represented by the standardbasketball positions, unless otherwise noted.

In essence, 1 is thepoint guard; 2 is theshooting guard; 3 is thesmall forward; 4 is thepower forward; 5 is thecenter.

sections.heading: Slot cut 1
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: Basic example of a slot cut executed by the power forward.]

[Image Alt Text: Basic example of a slot cut executed by the power forward.]

For this example, the offensive team is initially set up in a standard4 out 1 inalignment. In essence, 1 and 4 occupy theslots, 2 and 3 fill thewings, and 5 covers thelow postarea.

Also, 1 has initial possession of the basketball, represented by the black dot.

To begin, 1 passes the ball to 3, represented by the dotted arrow. Next, 3 makes a post entry pass with 5 as the recipient.

From there, 4 executes a slot cut towards the rim, receives the ball from 5, and has an opportunity to score afield goalvia alayupor dunk.

sections.heading: Slot cut 2
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: Basic example of a slot cut executed by the small forward.]

[Image Alt Text: Basic example of a slot cut executed by the small forward.]

This is yet another simple example that demonstrates a slot cut. For this sequence, there will be movement between three of the five players on the court.

1 starts near the left slot area adjacent to the three-point line. 2 begins near the left side wing with possession of the basketball.

3 is currently on theweak sidein the extended right slot area that is closer to the sideline but still above and outside of the wing.

4 fills the left side low post area while 5 occupies thedunker spotthat is near thebaselineon the weak side.

In terms of the offensive action, 2 performs a basic dribble drive towards the baseline by way of thestep-up screenset by 4.

While that occurs, 3 performs the slot cut towards the rim, receives the ball from 2, and has the opportunity to score points via a layup or dunk.

It should also be noted that when making this particular type of cut, 3 should try to time it so that the cut is in sequence with thedribble penetrationfrom 2.

Basically, 3 should immediately begin cutting to the basket via the slot at the moment when 2 dribbles towards the lane line on the left side, which is near the baseline as well.

What’s more, during the basket cut action, 3 should attempt to make contact with the high post elbow area or at least try to stay as close as possible to it.

The reason that can be a factor is because 3 would be cutting behind their defender who would probably be in ahelp sideposition near thenailarea, which is essentially the middle of the high post for the defense.

In other words, if 3 were to cut in towards the middle of the high post, especially too early from the slot, then their defender would probably see them and make an adjustment by getting in front of them or at least tagging them.

As a result, that defender would most likely be able to slow down the cutting action or completely hinder it.
